How to Use the Single-cell Schemas Website
Quick Navigation
  • Introduction
  • Why is only partial Sample metadata shown?
  • Single-cell RNA Sequencing overview
  • Spatial Transcriptomics images overview
  • REMBI overview

Introduction

The Single-cell Schemas website is developed by the Collaborative OPen Omics (COPO) project team based in Norwich, United Kingdom, to support the following research areas:

  • Single-cell Ribonucleic Acid Sequencing (Single-cell RNA Seq)
  • Images with a focus on:
    • Spatial Transcriptomics Fluorescence In Situ Hybridisation (ST FISH)
    • Recommended Metadata for Biological Images (REMBI)

Follow these steps to navigate the site effectively:

  1. Select a standard and a technology from the dropdown menus on the left of the web page.
  2. If data is available for your selection, the contents in the centre of the web page will automatically update to reflect the matching data.
  3. If no data is available, a modal will notify you.
  4. You can download a blank manifest related to your selection if data is available for it.

Why is only partial Sample metadata shown?

The Sample worksheet in each Single-cell manifest contains only a minimal set of metadata fields. Similarly, the Sample accordion on this Single-cell website displays only the recommended fields based on the chosen standard and technology. This is designed to help users focus on the essential information needed for their single-cell experiments.

However, many other sample-related metadata fields may be required, depending on the sample type to submit. If you are uploading data via COPO, we recommend that you first complete and upload the relevant sample manifest. This ensures that all necessary fields — such as biosampleAccession, scientific name, organism and taxon ID - are associated with a valid, previously submitted sample.

For a full list of available sample manifests, please visit the Manifests page on the Collaborative OPen Omics (COPO) website. For more information on Single-cell submissions, please refer to the Single-cell submissions guide .

Please note that the blank manifests available on the Manifests page do not contain any pre-filled sample metadata. If you have already submitted samples through COPO and received BioSample accessions, log in to COPO and navigate to the Single-cell component. From there, you can download a blank manifest with pre-filled sample metadata specific to your submission.

Navigate through the sections below to understand how the website integrates different combinations of standards and technologies.

Single-cell Ribonucleic Acid Sequencing (scRNA-seq) is a technique that allows researchers to profile gene expression at the single-cell level. Below are the key combinations of standards and technologies used in this field:

    Spatial Transcriptomics Fluorescence In Situ Hybridisation (STX FISH or ST FISH) maps gene expression within tissue sections by visualising specific RNA molecules with fluorescent probes, preserving their spatial context. Here are the key combinations of standards and technologies used in this area:

      REcommended Metadata for Biological Images (REMBI) is the standard used for General image submissions.

        Still have questions? Contact us for help.
